# Limitations

<!-- Status: REVISED 2026-06-04 -->

**LLM-as-judge and same-model family.** The citation-faithfulness scores come from a `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` judge evaluating answers generated by a Gemini-family answer model. Same-family judges tend to be lenient toward outputs from their own model family \cite{zheng2023judging}. The true citation_support may be lower than 0.978 under a human annotator or a cross-family judge (e.g., Claude or GPT-4o). Future work should validate a random sample of judgments against human ratings.

**Pilot benchmark size.** gold_40 v0.3 is 40 questions. Statistical uncertainty is high — one question is 2.5% of R@5. Results in the multi_hop category (n=5) and adversarial category (n=2) are especially noisy. Confidence intervals are not reported; with n=5, a 95% CI on multi_hop R@5=0.800 would span roughly ±0.30.

**Constructed benchmark, not user-sampled.** Questions were written by the development team to target known failure modes, not sampled from real users. The distribution of categories (30% thematic, 25% direct, 17.5% interpretive) reflects stress-testing priorities rather than user query distribution. Real-world R@5 may differ substantially.

**Single translation (BSB).** All verses come from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB), an evangelical Protestant translation first published in 2016 that is close in register and vocabulary to the ESV, with open licensing that permits redistribution. Results may not generalize to systems using KJV, NIV, ESV, or other translations, where verse wording differs and embeddings would shift accordingly.

**IVFFlat non-determinism.** The IVFFlat index used for approximate nearest-neighbor search is non-deterministic near its rank boundary. Across three repeated runs of v3 on gold_40 v0.2, three questions showed R@5 variance: aion_006 (interpretive, 2/3 runs pass), aion_007 (multi_hop, 2/3 runs pass), and aion_036 (multi_hop, 1/3 runs pass). Run-to-run R@5 ranged from 0.853 to 0.882 on the same question set and system. The main benchmark runs (v2 on v0.2, v3 on v0.3) were each performed once and frozen as canonical JSONL artifacts; results are reproducible from those frozen files. Live rerunning may produce different retrieval lists for boundary cases. Stability of the v0.3 run has not been tested across multiple runs; the headline R@5=0.941 is from a single frozen execution.

**No ablation study.** The paper reports v3 as a whole system. Individual contributions of each retrieval layer (exact lookup, chapter-aware query, per-chapter guarantee, semantic re-ranking) are not isolated. The v1→v2→v3 progression provides coarse evidence that each change improved performance, but without controlled ablation the relative contribution of each component is not quantified.

**Model versioning.** Both the answer model and judge model are identified by name (`gemini-3.1-flash-lite`) but not by a pinned checkpoint or API version. If Google updates the underlying weights of this model between the benchmark run and a reader's replication attempt, answer quality and judge scores may differ. Results are reproducible from the frozen JSONL artifacts, not from a live rerun.

**Dataset accessibility.** Aion-BibleQA is released at \url{https://github.com/Raoof128/Aion}. The benchmark JSONL files are self-contained; the `bible_verses` table (BSB text, stored in Supabase) is not redistributed, but the BSB text is available under open license and can be independently loaded into a pgvector-enabled Postgres instance using the schema documented in the repository.
